commit.message:
Load most recent candidates first in Talent Pool

**Change:** Fetch and sort candidates by created_at DESC before loading profiles, so the most recent 100 candidates load first instead of random order.

**Flow:**
1. Get candidate IDs from RPC
2. Fetch id + created_at timestamps (lightweight - 2 fields only)
3. Sort by created_at DESC (most recent first)
4. Load first 100 (newest candidates)
5. Continue loading remaining profiles from newest to oldest

**UX:** Users now see the most recent uploads first (~1-2 sec), then older candidates load progressively in background.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Phase 1.5 — Hub API + hook telemetry (port 8200)

- hub/db.py: SQLite telemetry.db schema (heartbeats, hook_events),
  WAL mode, 30-day retention, read-only + read-write contexts
- hub/models.py: Pydantic models for all API requests/responses
- hub/server.py: FastAPI with lifespan, 5 endpoints:
  GET /api/health, POST /api/heartbeat, GET /api/machines,
  POST /api/telemetry/hook, GET /api/telemetry/events,
  GET /api/telemetry/summary
- hub/reporter.py: fire-and-forget hook telemetry utility (0.2s timeout,
  silent fail — safe to call from hook scripts)
- collectors/telemetry.py: TUI reads telemetry.db read-only on each refresh
- widgets/hook_panel.py: shows last-fired ts + success rate per hook
  when hub is running; gracefully degrades to error-log-only when hub is off
- app.py: wires telemetry collector into refresh cycle
- tests/test_hub.py: 9 new tests — 24/24 passing

Start hub: .venv/Scripts/python -m memory_pulse.hub
Or:        .venv/Scripts/python -m uvicorn memory_pulse.hub.server:app --port 8200

commit.message:
fix: use Docker DNS resolver in nginx to survive container restarts

Add resolver 127.0.0.11 and use variables in proxy_pass for all Docker
service upstreams. This forces nginx to re-resolve hostnames every 10s
instead of caching IPs from startup, preventing 502 errors when
containers are rebuilt with new IPs.

fix: restore orchestration/dashboard with sanitized IPs

PROBLEM: Security commit (4f65645) used `git rm` instead of `git rm --cached`,
deleting all orchestration/ and dashboard/ files from disk AND tracking.
All hardcoded private IPs (192.168.100.x, 10.0.0.x) were the original concern.

SOLUTION: Restored files from git history, replaced ALL hardcoded IPs with
environment variables (ORCH_REDIS_HOST, ORCH_NEO4J_HOST, ORCH_NCCL_HOST,
ORCH_DASHBOARD_URL, ORCH_SSH_SPARK3, ORCH_SSH_THOR, ORCH_NODES_JSON, etc.).
Updated .gitignore to allow sanitized files back into tracking.
Added sidecar.py (Redis Streams consumer daemon).

TESTING: grep -rn for IP patterns returns zero matches in orchestration/ dashboard/

Fix data parsing for Apple Ads API response

The API returns granularity as an array of day objects, each with its
own date and metrics. Previously we only read the first day's data.

Now iterates through all granularity entries to save each day separately.

Also fixed metric field names:
- totalInstalls instead of installs
- totalNewDownloads instead of newDownloads
- totalRedownloads instead of redownloads
- totalInstallRate instead of conversionRate
- totalAvgCPI instead of avgCPA

commit.message:
feat: integrate SourceFilterEvaluator into Telegram bot connector [bu-qbq.8]

Adds chat_id-based source filter gating to the Telegram bot connector,
mirroring the existing pattern used in the Gmail connector.

Changes:
- source_filter.py: add extract_telegram_filter_key() helper that returns
  str(chat.id) for chat_id key_type; returns '' for all other types so the
  evaluator's unknown-type skip logic handles them correctly. Add to __all__.
- telegram_bot.py: import SourceFilterEvaluator and extract_telegram_filter_key;
  add TYPE_CHECKING import for asyncpg; accept optional db_pool in __init__;
  instantiate SourceFilterEvaluator(connector_type='telegram-bot'); call
  ensure_loaded() in both start_polling() and start_webhook() before ingestion
  begins; apply filter gate in _process_update() — blocked updates are dropped
  silently with no Switchboard call (update_id already advanced by getUpdates).
- test_telegram_bot_connector.py: add source filter test section covering
  extract_telegram_filter_key for private/group/edited/channel/non-message
  updates; blacklist blocks specific chat; whitelist allows only listed chats;
  non-chat_id key types don't block (domain filter gets empty key, no match);
  truly unknown key types emit WARNING and pass through; blocked update never
  calls Switchboard; ensure_loaded() called before polling loop; evaluator
  instantiated with connector_type='telegram-bot'.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: rename workflow to Dependency Updater (#35)

## What changed

Renamed the agentic workflow from "NuGet Package Update" to "Dependency
Updater" in both the workflow name and the markdown heading.

## Why

User requested a more general name.

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify workflow appears as "Dependency Updater" in the Actions tab
after merge

This maintains smooth professional flow while keeping all analysis-based descriptions intact.

commit.message:
refactor: replace hardcoded integration test schema with migration-runner [bu-8a1] (#495)

refactor: replace hardcoded CORE_TABLES_SQL with migration-runner fixture [bu-8a1]

Remove CORE_TABLES_SQL and SWITCHBOARD_TABLES_SQL constants from
tests/integration/test_integration.py and replace the _make_pool() helper
with one that uses the real Alembic migration runner (butlers.migrations.run_migrations).

This eliminates schema drift: integration tests now always exercise exactly
the schema that the production daemon applies at startup. The change was
discovered during bu-0b7.4 implementation when the hardcoded SQL was already
behind on multiple migrations (missing route_inbox, butler_secrets,
sessions.request_id NOT NULL constraint introduced by core_021, etc.).

Also fixes three session_create() callsites that omitted request_id, which
would have failed against the real schema (core_021 made the column NOT NULL).

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Risk-tiered position limits: max positions, bucket concentration, margin caps

Conservative: 2 positions, 1 per bucket, halt at 25% margin utilization
Moderate: 4 positions, 2 per bucket, halt at 50% margin utilization
Aggressive: 5 positions, 3 per bucket, halt at 75% margin utilization

Prevents cross-margin cascading liquidation on correlated altcoin dumps.

commit.message:
fix: explicit newline removal for CodeQL log-injection barrier (#703)

CodeQL cannot statically determine that the regex [^ -~\t] strips
\r\n characters. Add an explicit replace(/[\r\n]/g, '') step that
CodeQL recognizes as a log-injection sanitizer barrier, resolving
alerts #283 and #284.

commit.message:
feat(ui): wire up click handlers for extraction preview table (#663)

## Summary

- Wire up row and column click handlers in the extraction preview
overlay (`handleOverlayClick`)
- Wire up tab click handlers for extraction preview tabs (`ext-tab-N`
zones)
- Row clicks select the row and update the column cursor based on X
position (consistent with main table UX)
- Column header clicks select the column
- Tab clicks switch the preview group and reset row/column cursors
- Clicks are no-ops when not in explore mode
- Use distinct `ext-tab-N`/`ext-row-N`/`ext-col-N` zone prefixes for
extraction preview to avoid colliding with main table zones during
`overlay.Composite`
- Add `colZonePrefix`/`rowZonePrefix` parameters to `renderHeaderRow`
and `renderRows` so callers choose their zone namespace
- Fix `requireZone` test helper to call `View()` twice so the async zone
worker processes markers before `Get()` — previously zone-based click
tests were silently skipping
- Five new tests covering tab click, row click, column click, row+column
selection, and non-explore-mode guard

closes #628

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
